title: Fiber Model
description: Fibers are CReact's internal representation of the component tree. Each component instance maps to a fiber node.
what_is_fiber_body: A fiber is a unit of work in the render tree. Each component instance in your JSX corresponds to a fiber node. Fibers form a tree that mirrors your component hierarchy.
step_creation_body: when JSX is evaluated, fibers are created for each component
step_reconciliation_body: fibers are compared against the previous state
step_execution_body: handlers run for new or changed fibers
step_serialization_body: fiber outputs are saved to Memory
step_cleanup_body: removed fibers have their cleanup handlers called
paths_body: Each fiber has a path, an array of string segments from the root to that fiber. Instance nodes derive their ID by joining the path with "." (e.g., app.aws.web-site-blog). Paths are used to match nodes across runs for reconciliation.
instance_nodes_body: Fibers represent the component tree structure. When a component calls useAsyncOutput, it creates an InstanceNode that is attached to the fiber via instanceNodes. Instance nodes are what the reconciler diffs and the runtime deploys. They carry the handler, outputs, and cleanup function.
source_body: The fiber interface and createFiber live in runtime/src/fiber.ts. Instance nodes are defined in runtime/src/instance.ts. Rendering logic is in runtime/src/render.ts.
